title: Replace tar extraction with in-process JS implementation
description: Walkthrough of changes replacing execSync('tar -xzf ...') with an in-process extractTarGz function using Node.js built-in modules, fixing the Windows GNU tar colon-interpretation failure.

steps.title: New extractTarGz function — the core fix
steps.description: This is the new in-process tar extraction function that replaces the execSync('tar -xzf ...') call. It reads the .tgz file, decompresses with gunzipSync, then parses tar entries in a loop over 512-byte blocks. Uses only node:zlib, node:fs, and node:path — zero third-party dependencies.

steps.title: Tar header parsing helpers
steps.description: Helper functions for reading null-terminated ASCII strings, octal numbers, computing checksums, and detecting zero blocks from tar headers. These implement the USTAR tar format spec at the byte level.

steps.title: Header field parsing and validation
steps.description: Parses the key header fields: name (bytes 0-99), size (bytes 124-135), typeflag (byte 156), and USTAR prefix (bytes 345-499). Validates the stored checksum against a computed checksum to detect corruption.

steps.title: Path traversal protection
steps.description: Security check: strips leading slashes, rejects entries containing '..' path components, and validates the resolved path starts with the destination directory. Prevents zip-slip style attacks on the extraction path.

steps.title: Entry type handling
steps.description: Handles three entry types: directories (typeflag '5') via mkdirSync, regular files (typeflag '0' or null) via writeFileSync, and PAX extended headers (typeflag 'x' or 'g') which are silently skipped. Other types (symlinks, etc.) are also skipped for safety.

steps.title: Import the new extraction function
steps.description: Added import for extractTarGz from the new extract.ts module. The execSync import at line 1 is deliberately retained — it's still used by copyDirRecursive (line 34) for the EXDEV cross-filesystem fallback.

steps.title: Replacement of the bug site
steps.description: The original execSync('tar -xzf "${tarballPath}" -C "${stagingDir}"') is replaced with extractTarGz(tarballPath, stagingDir). The try/catch error handling structure is preserved exactly — extraction errors are caught and returned as { success: false, error: 'Extraction failed: ...' }.

steps.title: Test helper: programmatic tarball creation
steps.description: The makeTarHeader and createTestTarGz helpers build valid USTAR-format tar archives programmatically using gzipSync. This avoids opaque binary fixture files and keeps tests self-contained with zero test-only dependencies.

steps.title: Test 1: Representative CI-shaped tarball
steps.description: Creates a .tgz with the exact structure the CI workflow produces (dist/index.js, skill-content/SKILL.md, package.json, build-metadata.json), extracts it, and verifies all files exist with correct content.

steps.title: Test 2: Colon-in-path — validates the original failure mode
steps.description: Extracts to a directory with a colon in its name (staging:test), directly validating the original GNU tar failure mode where C: was interpreted as a remote host. This test confirms the fix works because no external tar binary is involved.

steps.title: Test 3: Corrupt tarball error handling
steps.description: Passes garbage bytes as a .tgz file and asserts that extractTarGz throws an error. This validates the error contract — performStagedUpdate's try/catch catches the throw and returns { success: false, error }.
